name: 37mm Anti-Aircraft Gun
prompt:
Vietnam War era Soviet 61-K 37mm automatic anti-aircraft cannon. Medium-altitude NVA air defense.

Physical structure:
- Barrel: single long 37mm barrel (~2.5m), with muzzle brake (two-port)
  - Flash hider at muzzle
  - Barrel jacket/sleeve around breech area
- Receiver: large breech/receiver mechanism
  - Ammunition clips feed from top (5-round clips)
  - Ejection port on side
- Cradle: gun mounts on elevation cradle
  - Trunnions for elevation (-5 to +85 degrees)
  - Balance spring housing
- Turntable: 360-degree traverse mount
  - Central pedestal/post
  - Platform base
- Seats: two seats (gunner and loader), one each side
  - Shoulder rests with control wheels
  - Elevation handwheel
  - Traverse handwheel
- Legs: 4 outrigger stabilizer legs, deployed in X pattern
  - Ground pads at ends
- Shield: small curved armor shield in front of gunner position (optional)
- Ammunition box: ammo can near loader position

Colors: Military dark green (0x3d4a2a), gun steel (0x3a3a3a), darker metal (0x2a2a2a), seats (0x555555)
Orientation: Barrel pointing +Z, elevated ~30 degrees, deployed on ground
Scale: Real-world meters (~4m long including barrel, ~2m tall in firing position)
animations: Barrel elevation: elevates from 30 to 80 degrees over 3 seconds, then back
